blockingIssues.description: WriteFileTool lacks workspace path validation - critical security gap. WriteFileTool can write to any path on the filesystem (e.g., /etc/passwd, ~/.ssh/), unlike ReadFileTool which properly validates paths are within workspace using canonicalize(). The WriteFileTool struct is missing workspace_path field, with_workspace_path() builder method, and validate_path() method.

symbol_search patterns don't match common Rust syntax: patterns like '^fn {} ' fail to match 'async fn', 'pub fn', 'extern fn', 'unsafe fn', and generic functions 'fn foo<T>()'. Also, macro_rules! pattern is missing '!' (uses colon instead of exclamation).

PermissionTier::Ask is treated identically to Deny - both result in blocking execution. The feature requires 'Ask confirmation for medium' but no confirmation mechanism exists. Tools with Ask tier simply fail. No dedicated permission_tiers.rs module exists - system is distributed across executor.rs, hybrid.rs, tools.rs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that tools implementation is in crates/swell-tools/src/tools.rs, not separate files like file.rs, git.rs. Workers looked for git.rs, file.rs but implementation is consolidated in tools.rs.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tools-file-read, tools-file-write, tools-git-ops all had task description pointing to non-existent files

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add boundary rule: When implementing file tools, both ReadFileTool AND WriteFileTool must have consistent workspace path validation. Workers should not mark WriteFileTool complete without path validation just because ReadFileTool has it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tools-file-write worker marked 'fully implemented' but missing critical security feature present in sibling ReadFileTool

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add boundary rule: symbol_search patterns for Rust must handle modifiers (async, pub, extern, unsafe) and generics. Regex anchoring with '^' at line start fails when modifiers precede 'fn'. Pattern should be '^\s*(pub\s+)?(async\s+)?(extern\s+)?(unsafe\s+)?fn\s+' to handle all combinations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tools-search worker didn't catch that patterns only match bare 'fn ' without modifiers

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add boundary rule: PermissionTier::Ask requires a confirmation mechanism, not just blocking. If Ask is implemented, there must be a way to confirm or deny. If confirmation cannot be implemented, Ask should fall back to Auto (auto-approve) rather than silently treating as Deny (block).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tools-permission-tiers worker marked 'fully implemented' but Ask tier has no confirmation flow, making it identical to Deny

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Standardize skill names in features.json - 'rust-worker' skill does not exist. Actual skill is 'Rust'. Document canonical skill names to prevent worker confusion.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tools-search, tools-permission-tiers, tools-shell-exec, tools-file-read all referenced non-existent 'rust-worker' skill

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker noting rust-worker skill doesn't exist is duplicate - covered by systematic skill name mismatch suggestion above
